title: Transparent metallo-dielectric coatings, structures, and devices, and methods of fabrication thereof
abstract: Various embodiments of the present disclosure provide metallo- dielectric low-emissivity coatings, structures coated with such low-emissivity coatings, and methods of fabrication thereof. The metallo-dielectric coatings are formed from a visibly-transparent, and heat-reflective, multi-layer structure including an transparent amorphous substrate having a nano-thin metal layer adhered thereto, wherein the nano-thin metal layer is adhered to a transparent amorphous substrate via a transparent dielectric seeding layer. The transparent amorphous dielectric layer may include amorphous carbon. Various example embodiments are disclosed in which such coatings are employed as passive or active devices, such as transparent heat mirrors, transparent resistive heaters for windows and windshields, transparent electromagnetically isolating security films, near invisible radio transceivers and transmitters.
claims:
Claims THEREFORE WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A metallo-dielectric coating comprising: a transparent amorphous dielectric layer; a transparent dielectric seeding layer formed on the transparent amorphous dielectric layer; and a nano-thin metal layer formed on the transparent dielectric seeding layer; and a transparent dielectric film formed on the nano-thin metal layer; wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer is selected such that the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a continuous surface in the absence of isolated islands. 2. The metallo-dielectric coating according to claim 1 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness between 3 nm and 20 nm. 3. The metallo-dielectric coating according to claim 1 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness between 3 nm and 10 nm. 4. The metallo-dielectric coating according to claim 1 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness between 3 nm and 7 nm. 5. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer and the transparent amorphous dielectric layer are selected such that a heat of formation of 

 chemical reactions between atomic constituents of the transparent dielectric seeding layer and the transparent amorphous dielectric layer are less than - 100 kJ/mol. 6. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 5 wherein a lattice parameter of the exposed crystal face of the transparent dielectric seeding layer differs by less than 12.5% from a scalar multiple of a lattice parameter of the nano-thin metal layer. 7. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 6 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer exhibits a higher surface tension than the nano-thin metal layer. 8. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 7 wherein the transparent amorphous dielectric layer is formed from amorphous carbon. 9. The metallo-dielectric coating according to claim 8 wherein the amorphous carbon is polymeric-like hydrogenated amorphous carbon. 0. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 9 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed from silver. 1 1 . The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 1 0 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer is formed from AIN. 12. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 1 1 further comprising a dielectric film formed on the nano-thin metal layer. 13. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 1 2 wherein the transparent amorphous dielectric layer has varying optical properties across its thickness. 14. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 1 3 wherein a thickness of one or more layers are selected to generate optical interference such that the metallo-dielectric coating is transmissive in a visible spectral region and reflective in an infrared spectral region. 15. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 14 further comprising electrical contacts in electrical communication with the nano-thin metal layer. 16. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 1 5 wherein the metallo-dielectric coating is formed on a transparent substrate. 17. A metallo-dielectric structure comprising: a transparent substrate; a first transparent dielectric seeding layer formed on the transparent substrate; a first nano-thin metal layer formed on the first transparent dielectric 

 seeding layer; a first transparent amorphous dielectric layer formed on the first nano- thin metal layer; a second transparent dielectric seeding layer formed on the first transparent amorphous dielectric layer; a second nano-thin metal layer formed on the second transparent dielectric seeding layer; a second transparent amorphous dielectric layer formed on the second nano-thin metal layer; and a capping dielectric layer formed on the second transparent amorphous dielectric layer; wherein the first nano-thin metal layer and the second nano-thin metal layer are intrinsically transparent within at least a portion of the visible spectrum, and wherein the first nano-thin metal layer and the second nano- thin metal layer are formed with a continuous surface in the absence of isolated islands. 18. A metallo-dielectric coating comprising: a transparent amorphous dielectric layer; and a nano-thin metal layer formed on the transparent amorphous dielectric layer; and a transparent dielectric film formed on the nano-thin metal layer; wherein the nano-thin metal layer is intrinsically transparent within at least a portion of the visible spectrum; and wherein the transparent amorphous dielectric layer is doped with a 

 dopant selected to produce a surface such that the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a continuous surface in the absence of isolated islands. 19. The metallo-dielectric coating according to claim 18 wherein the transparent amorphous dielectric layer is amorphous carbon, and the dopant is selected from the group consisting of Ag, Al and N atoms. 20. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 18 to 19 wherein a thickness of one or more layers are selected to generate optical interference such that the metallo-dielectric coating is transmissive in a visible spectral region and reflective in an infrared spectral region. 21. The metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of any one of claims 18 to 20 further comprising electrical contacts in electrical communication with the nano-thin metal layer. 22. A method of fabricating a metallo-dielectric coating, the method comprising: depositing, onto a transparent substrate, a transparent amorphous dielectric layer; depositing a transparent dielectric seeding layer onto the transparent amorphous dielectric layer; and depositing a nano-thin metal layer on the transparent dielectric seeding layer, the nano-thin metal layer forming a continuous surface in the absence of isolated islands; 

 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness such that the nano-thin metal layer is intrinsically transparent within at least a portion of the visible spectrum. 23. The method according to claim 22 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer is formed from a material selected such that a contact angle between the metal, during the deposition of the nano-thin metal layer, is sufficiently low to support even spreading of the metal and to avoid the formation of isolated islands. 24. The method according to claim 22 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness between 3 nm and 20 nm. 25. The method according to claim 22 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness between 3 nm and 10 nm. 26. The method according to claim 22 wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness between 3 nm and 7 nm. 27. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 26 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer and the transparent amorphous dielectric layer are formed from materials selected such that a heat of formation of chemical reactions between atomic constituents of the transparent dielectric seeding layer and the transparent amorphous dielectric layer are less than - 100 kJ/mol. 28. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 27 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer is formed such that an exposed crystal face thereof is characterized by a lattice parameter differing by less than 12.5% from a scalar multiple of a lattice parameter of the nano-thin metal layer. 29. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 28 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer is formed to exhibit a higher surface tension than the nano-thin metal layer. 30. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 29 wherein the transparent amorphous dielectric layer is formed from amorphous carbon. 31 . The method according to claim 30 wherein the amorphous carbon is polymeric-like hydrogenated amorphous carbon. 32. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 31 wherein the nano- thin metal layer is formed from silver. 33. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 32 wherein the transparent dielectric seeding layer is formed from AIN. 34. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 33 further comprising forming a dielectric film on the nano-thin metal layer. 35. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 34 further comprising irradiating the transparent dielectric seeding layer during deposition of the transparent dielectric seeding layer. 36. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 35 further wherein two or more layers are formed within a sputtering chamber by employing a sputtering target containing multiple targets. 37. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 36 further comprising controlling a thickness of one or more layers to generate optical interference such that the metallo-dielectric coating is transmissive in a visible spectral region and reflective in an infrared spectral region. 38. The method according to any one of claims 22 to 37 further comprising forming electrical contacts in electrical communication with the nano-thin metal layer. 39. A method of fabricating a metallo-dielectric coating, the method comprising: depositing, onto a transparent substrate, a transparent amorphous dielectric layer; doping the transparent amorphous dielectric layer with a dopant; depositing a nano-thin metal layer on the transparent amorphous dielectric layer; and 

 depositing a transparent dielectric film on the nano-thin metal layer; wherein the dopant is selected such that the nano-thin metal layer is formed on the transparent amorphous dielectric layer with a continuous surface in the absence of isolated islands; and wherein the nano-thin metal layer is formed with a thickness such that the nano-thin metal layer is intrinsically transparent within at least a portion of the visible spectrum. 40. The method according to claim 39 wherein the transparent amorphous dielectric layer is amorphous carbon, and the dopant is selected from the group consisting of Ag, Al and N atoms. 41 . The method according to claim 39 or 40 further comprising controlling a thickness of one or more layers to generate optical interference such that the metallo-dielectric coating is transmissive in a visible spectral region and reflective in an infrared spectral region. 42. The method according to any one of claims 39 to 41 further comprising forming electrical contacts in electrical communication with the nano-thin metal layer. 43. A solar control window comprising a metallo-dielectric coating according to any one of claims 1 to 21 .
